concerns_addressed: Commit-ordering note investigated: the actual T4 AgentPage deliverable IS present and correctly attributed. T4's spec commit (e422568) is the direct parent of the T4 merge commit (debbe3c7). The assigned SHA range git diff --stat surfaces only routes.ts/routes.test.ts because of merge-commit symmetric-difference behavior, NOT because the deliverable is missing. Verified final-state files directly: AgentPage.tsx and AgentPage.test.tsx contain the full follow-up UI. No T4 deliverable is missing or mis-attributed.

Verified must-not-have: client/src/lib/agentColumnState.ts is unchanged across the phase — the __notfirst__ exclusion is done in AgentPage via the columnAgentEvents filter, not by modifying deriveColumnState (T5's scope).

strengths: All 7 DELIVERABLE scenarios verified in code and covered by 12 passing tests (AgentPage.test.tsx) — input enable/disable, __notfirst__ chat bubbles, NEW_DIRECTION confirm/cancel buttons with correct structured payloads, and __notfirst__ exclusion from column tiles.

__notfirst__ exclusion implemented cleanly via a single memoized columnAgentEvents filter (lines 648-651) fed to AgentBoardVisual, satisfying the CRITICAL invariant without touching deriveColumnState.
Follow-up streaming text is boardId-scoped (lines 656-661), preventing cross-board token leakage.
Regenerate handlers guard on activeWorkspaceId + board + pendingRegenerate, use the boardRef pattern consistent with the existing queue/createBoard architecture, and handle errors with showToast.
Input placeholder/disabled logic correctly distinguishes running vs pending-regeneration vs follow-up vs refine states (lines 1000-1011, 681-685).

issues.description: followUpMessages rendered with array index as React key. Acceptable for an append-only list that is never reordered or spliced, but a stable id would be more robust if the list ever supports edits.
